audit.dirty_worktree: Worktree changed during the audit. Final validation status showed modified CHANGELOG.md, docs/changelog/0.196.md, docs/design/0.196-sqlite-comparison-audit/implementation-results.json, docs/design/0.196-sqlite-comparison-audit/implementation-results.md, testing/integration/tests/sql_perf_matrix_audit.rs, plus the new docs/audits/reports/2026-07/2026-07-04 audit artifacts from this task.

commands_run.notes: Environmental PocketIC binary missing for fluent_perf_audit; focused approved rerun passed.

commands_run.command: env IC_TESTKIT_ALLOW_POCKET_IC_DOWNLOAD=1 cargo test -p icydb-testing-integration --test fluent_perf_audit
targeted rg/find/sed inspections recorded in markdown report

commands_run.notes: Final artifact counts: report 1900 lines, ledger 1003 lines, total 2903 lines.
Final status recorded modified pre-existing files plus untracked technical-debt audit directory.
Final artifact counts: report 1904 lines, ledger 1032 lines, total 2936 lines.

summary.is_debt_accumulating: Mostly managed. The repo is paying down major architecture debt, but pre-1.0 hardening debt is accumulating around public DTOs, durable byte formats, recovery/migration boundaries, query contracts, and 0.197 proof/cache tests.
top_immediate_cleanup_prs: Update query/cursor contracts for 0.196 route-dependent pushdown facts.
Refactor SQL perf matrix route classification into typed metadata.
Refresh docs/1.0-TODO.md and stale 0.196 status text.
top_pre_1_0_blockers: Durable migration lifecycle or explicit broad-migration non-goal.
Public EXPLAIN/SQL/perf DTO stability and version policy.
Recovery-size support matrix or streaming recovery design.
summary.biggest_architecture_debt: Planner/admission/executor proof ownership around 0.197 primary-key canonicalization.
summary.biggest_test_debt: Missing 0.197 proof/cache/parity tests plus remaining fast-path tripwire gaps.
summary.biggest_doc_debt: Query/cursor contracts and docs/1.0-TODO.md lag current design status.
summary.biggest_performance_debt: Recursive structural decode allocation and missing allocation/wasm-size measurements.
summary.biggest_api_debt: Public EXPLAIN, SQL result, and perf attribution DTOs may harden before their stability policy is explicit.

findings.title: Query/cursor performance contract lags 0.196 route facts
evidence: docs/contracts/QUERY_CONTRACT.md states cursor continuation is post-access and not pushed into index seek/range operations.
docs/contracts/CURSOR.md repeats post-access behavior.
docs/design/0.196-sqlite-comparison-audit/implementation-results.md records pushed ordered-read and limit-stop route facts.
findings.why_debt: The docs blur semantic guarantees and current route-dependent optimization facts.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Pushdown should not be promised as a public cursor guarantee.
findings.risk_if_ignored: 0.197 implementers may rely on stale performance wording or users may misread diagnostics as token semantics.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Rewrite query/cursor performance sections to separate semantics from internal route pushdown.
acceptance_criteria: Docs cite 0.196 implementation results.
Pagination semantics remain unchanged.
Pushdown is documented as route-dependent optimization.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Update query and cursor contracts to reflect 0.196 route-dependent ordered-read pushdown without making it a public cursor guarantee.

findings.title: Primary-key equality canonicalization gap
evidence: docs/design/0.197-deterministic-optimizer-canonicalization/0.197-design.md requires scalar primary-key equality and finite IN canonicalization.
docs/contracts/READ_ADMISSION.md documents exact by_id/by_ids admission.
findings.why_debt: Users expect WHERE pk = value to converge with by_id(value).
findings.why_may_be_intentional: This is intentionally deferred from 0.196 into 0.197.
findings.risk_if_ignored: IcyDB-specific query spellings harden and later changes become more visible.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Implement planner-owned ByKey/ByKeys/Empty for strict scalar primary-key predicates only.
acceptance_criteria: SQL/fluent parity tests pass.
Wrong-type and malformed inputs fail without scanning.
Empty performs zero data/index IO.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Implement the first 0.197 scalar primary-key canonicalization slice with planner-owned proof artifacts and fail-closed tests.

findings.title: Planner/admission/executor proof drift risk
evidence: 0.197 design says canonicalization must not be a late executor trick.
docs/governance/fast-path-inventory.md lists unguarded areas.
crates/icydb-core/src/db/query/admission.rs is a large admission authority.
findings.why_debt: Independent proof inference can make admission, explain, cache, and execution diverge.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Existing exact-read and 0.196 pushdown paths are deliberately bounded.
findings.risk_if_ignored: A future optimization could become a public-read bypass or stale explain/cache route.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Add tests that force execution to consume a planner-owned proof artifact.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if executor independently upgrades a non-admitted shape.
Explain/admission/execution route facts agree for admitted shapes.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add 0.197 proof-drift guard tests before implementing primary-key canonicalization.

findings.title: Recursive structural-value decode allocates full owned values
evidence: crates/icydb-core/src/db/data/structural_field/value_storage/decode/value.rs has the only two active scoped source TODOs.
Both TODOs identify recursive list/map decode allocation.
findings.why_debt: Projection-only and validation paths cannot avoid full recursive Value allocation.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: The current path is correct, bounded, and simpler.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Row-codec and projection performance work may hide allocation costs.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Add measurement counters first, then consider a visitor path.
acceptance_criteria: Recursive decode allocation pressure is measured.
No persisted-format change is introduced.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add measurement-only recursive decode allocation counters and decide whether a visitor path is justified.

findings.title: Persisted format inventory is not a byte-level spec
evidence: docs/contracts/PERSISTED_FORMAT_INVENTORY.md says it is a review checklist, not a byte-level layout specification.
docs/contracts/PERSISTED_FORMAT_POLICY.md governs classifications but not exact bytes.
findings.why_debt: Post-1.0 compatibility review needs exact durable byte authority.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Before 1.0, source-owned encoding and hard cuts are acceptable.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Durable byte changes can land without complete compatibility review.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Write a byte-level persisted-format spec linked to codecs and tests.
acceptance_criteria: Every persisted surface is mapped to bytes, version behavior, and tests.
Unknown version behavior is explicit.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Create a byte-level persisted-format specification expanding the existing inventory.

findings.title: Schema mutation lacks durable migration lifecycle
evidence: 0.192 audit identified no durable migration IDs, phases, watermarks, or reentry state.
crates/icydb-core/src/db/schema/mutation/mod.rs says startup reconciliation can currently execute only no-rebuild plans.
SchemaFieldPathIndexRunner is narrow and does not by itself create a durable lifecycle.
findings.why_debt: Physical schema work needs recovery authority comparable to commit recovery.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Broad online migration may remain a 1.0 non-goal.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Future DDL can publish schema around incomplete physical work.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Implement minimal durable migration state or explicitly keep broad online migrations out of 1.0.
acceptance_criteria: Incomplete migration state fails closed.
Admitted physical migration paths are reentrant or explicitly unsupported.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Define the 1.0 schema migration boundary with durable phases or explicit fail-closed non-goal documentation.

findings.title: Recovery scale evidence is regression floor, not production bound
evidence: docs/contracts/DURABILITY.md says 0.190/0.191 recovery measurements are proof shapes, not production IC guarantees.
docs/operations/DURABILITY_GUIDE.md warns operators not to claim arbitrary large-index recovery is budget-certified.
findings.why_debt: Durability product claims need a supported recovery-size envelope.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Fail-closed recovery is the correct current posture.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Large canisters may fail closed without a documented support bound.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Produce a recovery-size support matrix or streaming recovery design.
acceptance_criteria: Supported recovery bounds or streaming design are documented.
Idempotent reentry tests cover the supported bound.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Produce recovery-size support evidence or a streaming recovery design with fail-closed tests.

findings.title: Fast-path ownership tripwires remain incomplete
evidence: docs/governance/fast-path-inventory.md lists stream precedence helpers, grouped dedicated ownership, and bytes-terminal derivation exception as unguarded.
findings.why_debt: Fast paths can silently drift from planner-owned proofs.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: The inventory explicitly tracks these as follow-ups.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Future optimization can bypass proof/admission boundaries.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Add one focused guard test per remaining unguarded area.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory is updated after tests land.
Tests fail on ownership/precedence drift.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add guard tests for the three remaining fast-path inventory gaps.

findings.title: SQL perf matrix route classifier relies on SQL string heuristics
evidence: testing/integration/tests/sql_perf_matrix_audit.rs is about 5563 lines.
route_classification_for_sample branches on sample.sql.contains(...) patterns.
Current dirty change adds GROUP BY string classification.
findings.why_debt: Performance reports can be misclassified if SQL text changes but execution facts do not.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: It is test-only and pragmatic for saved-report closeout.
findings.risk_if_ignored: 0.197 route deltas may be hidden as classifier changes.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Use structured scenario metadata and runtime route facts, keeping saved-report fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Current labels remain stable.
Classifier unit tests cover route families.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Refactor matrix route classification to structured metadata while preserving saved-report compatibility.

findings.title: Public EXPLAIN and SQL DTOs need stability policy
evidence: crates/icydb/src/db/query/mod.rs re-exports many Explain*V1 types.
ExplainAccessDecisionV1 has public fields and schema_version.
SqlQueryResult and SQL perf attribution DTOs expose public fields.
findings.why_debt: Route facts and diagnostics are still changing, but public DTOs can freeze internal vocabulary.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: V1 names and schema_version show versioning intent.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Future diagnostics cleanup becomes public API breakage.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Classify each DTO as stable, versioned, or opaque before 1.0.
acceptance_criteria: API docs state DTO stability guarantees.
Candid/API shape tests cover stable schemas.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Audit public EXPLAIN, SQL result, and perf attribution DTO stability before 1.0.

findings.title: 1.0 TODO mixes stale, closed, and real blockers
evidence: docs/1.0-TODO.md has many unchecked items that overlap with later contracts and changelogs.
findings.why_debt: Noisy release checklists hide current blockers and cause duplicated audits.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: It is an umbrella checklist rather than a status dashboard.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Release planning wastes effort re-proving already-closed work.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Reclassify entries as closed, blocker, non-goal, deferred, or needs evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Each remaining blocker links to evidence or owner doc.
Closed/non-goal items are separated.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Reconcile docs/1.0-TODO.md against current contracts and changelog evidence.

findings.title: Query-plan cache identity must hard-cut for 0.197 if shape semantics change
evidence: crates/icydb-core/src/db/session/query/cache.rs has SHARED_QUERY_PLAN_CACHE_METHOD_VERSION = 3.
0.197 design requires hard-cut cache identity if semantics change.
findings.why_debt: Primary-key predicate canonicalization changes the meaning of cached query shapes.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: The method-version mechanism is already the right mitigation.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Parameterized SQL can reuse stale values or stale generic/rejected plans.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Add parameterized cache identity tests before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Same shape with different key values returns the bound key.
Wrong type fails without scan.
Method-version change makes old entries miss.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add 0.197 query/SQL cache identity tests for shape/value separation and stale plan misses.

findings.title: Operator durability diagnostics remain narrower than recovery authority
evidence: 0.192 audit requested compact operator durability diagnostics.
Durability docs warn raw stable-memory access bypasses guarded recovery.
findings.why_debt: Operators need marker/journal/fold readiness facts without raw access.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Raw repair/import remains an explicit non-goal.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Recovery triage requires internal source knowledge.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Add bounded operator durability health report.
acceptance_criteria: Report is query-safe.
Clean, marker-present, journal-tail, and fail-closed states are tested.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Design and add a bounded durability health report for guarded recovery readiness facts.

findings.title: Perf evidence lacks allocation counters and wasm-size deltas
evidence: 0.196 implementation results state no wasm-size artifact was produced.
SQL perf matrix records instruction counters but not allocation counters.
findings.why_debt: Performance changes can move cost into allocation or code size without visibility.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Full matrices are already expensive and 0.196 was diagnostic-only.
findings.risk_if_ignored: 0.197 perf closeout may miss allocation or wasm regressions.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Add measurement-only wasm-size and allocation/proxy counters.
acceptance_criteria: Reports include raw wasm byte size where available.
Counters cover decode, projection, cache, and covering paths.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add measurement-only wasm-size and allocation/proxy counters to the SQL perf harness.

findings.title: Feature combination coverage exists but was not audited as fully closed
evidence: crates/icydb and crates/icydb-core have default=[], sql, sql-explain, diagnostics.
Makefile and CI include no-default and feature checks.
This audit ran all-features only.
findings.why_debt: All-features does not prove no-default or diagnostics-only behavior.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: CI appears to cover important combinations.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Release audits may overclaim feature coverage.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Document supported feature matrix and check mapping.
acceptance_criteria: Supported combinations are listed.
Each combination maps to CI/Makefile commands.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Document the supported feature matrix and its CI coverage.

findings.title: SQL/fluent parity for 0.197 is not yet proven
evidence: 0.197 design lists SQL literal, SQL parameter, and fluent primary-key equivalence tests as required.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: The behavior belongs to the 0.197 slice.
findings.risk_if_ignored: SQL and fluent behavior can diverge around exact primary-key access.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Add parity tests before or with canonicalization.
acceptance_criteria: SQL literal, SQL parameter, and fluent variants return same results and route facts.
Wrong-type and residual cases fail consistently.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add 0.197 SQL/fluent primary-key equality and IN parity tests.

findings.title: Macro/codegen public path assumptions need feature-combo smoke
evidence: crates/icydb/src/lib.rs exposes a doc-hidden __macro generated-code surface.
icydb-build emits feature-gated generated endpoint code.
findings.why_debt: Hidden facade paths can become de facto public for generated downstream code.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Generated code needs narrow hidden wiring.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Facade cleanup can break generated code under untested features.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Add compile fixtures for supported generated-code feature combinations.
acceptance_criteria: No-default, SQL, SQL-explain, and diagnostics generated-code paths compile where supported.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add generated-code compile fixtures for facade __macro paths across supported feature combinations.

findings.title: 0.196 design status header is stale versus implementation closeout
evidence: 0.196 design says full-matrix closeout is pending.
0.196 implementation results show full-matrix closeout evidence.
findings.why_debt: Stale status causes duplicated audit work.
findings.why_may_be_intentional: Design docs can preserve historical context.
findings.risk_if_ignored: Readers distrust or rerun already-recorded closeout evidence.
findings.recommended_cleanup: Update status header to point at implementation-results.md.
acceptance_criteria: Header no longer says closeout pending.
Implementation results are linked.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Update the 0.196 design status header to point at implementation-results.md as closeout authority.

backlog.title: Write byte-level persisted-format spec
acceptance_criteria: All durable surfaces mapped to bytes, versions, and tests.

backlog.title: Decide 1.0 schema migration boundary
backlog.expected_benefit: Prevents unsafe migration semantics from hardening.
acceptance_criteria: Durable phases exist or broad migrations are explicit non-goal.

backlog.title: Public DTO stability/version audit
acceptance_criteria: Each DTO classified stable, versioned, or opaque.

backlog.title: Recovery-size support matrix or streaming plan
acceptance_criteria: Supported recovery bounds or streaming design documented and tested.

backlog.title: Cursor contract closure
backlog.expected_benefit: Aligns semantics, tokens, and route optimization wording.
acceptance_criteria: Query and cursor contracts describe semantics separately from pushdown.

backlog.title: 0.197 cache identity tests
acceptance_criteria: Same-shape/different-value and wrong-type tests pass.

backlog.title: 0.197 SQL/fluent parity tests
acceptance_criteria: SQL literal, SQL parameter, and fluent forms match.

backlog.title: 0.197 proof-drift guard tests
acceptance_criteria: Execution cannot upgrade access without planner proof artifact.

backlog.title: Add remaining fast-path guards
acceptance_criteria: Fast-path inventory remaining areas are guarded.

backlog.title: Refactor SQL perf route classifier
acceptance_criteria: Structured metadata drives classification for generated scenarios.

backlog.title: Refresh docs/1.0-TODO.md
backlog.expected_benefit: Separates stale items from real blockers.
acceptance_criteria: Items are closed, blocker, non-goal, deferred, or needs-evidence.

backlog.title: Update 0.196 design status
acceptance_criteria: Status links to implementation-results.md.

backlog.title: Add wasm-size measurement
acceptance_criteria: Reports include raw wasm byte size when available.

backlog.title: Add allocation/proxy counters
acceptance_criteria: Decode, projection, cache, and covering paths have counters or proxies.

backlog.title: Recursive decode visitor experiment
backlog.expected_benefit: Can reduce projection decode cost if counters justify it.
acceptance_criteria: No persisted-format change; benchmark demonstrates benefit.

backlog.title: Operator durability health DTO
acceptance_criteria: Health report is query-safe and covers marker/journal/fold readiness.

backlog.title: Feature matrix contract
acceptance_criteria: Supported feature combinations map to CI/Makefile checks.

backlog.title: Generated-code feature fixtures
acceptance_criteria: No-default, SQL, SQL-explain, and diagnostics generated-code paths compile where supported.

backlog.title: Link contracts to tests
acceptance_criteria: Contracts cite core tests for major invariants.

backlog.title: Public SQL result version note
acceptance_criteria: SqlQueryResult version/stability policy documented and tested.

backlog.title: Explain route facts stability note
acceptance_criteria: EXPLAIN DTO stability and versioning documented.

backlog.title: Recovery fail-closed operator examples
acceptance_criteria: Operations guide includes marker/journal/recovery failure examples.

backlog.title: Heap vs journaled parity note
acceptance_criteria: Docs distinguish semantic parity from durability parity.

backlog.title: Matrix saved-artifact archiving policy
acceptance_criteria: Perf docs define when matrix artifacts must be archived.

backlog.title: Changelog/audit closeout checklist
acceptance_criteria: Governance docs describe how audit findings become backlog and closeout evidence.
